titleStmt.title: Letter from Hiram Shaw Wilkinson to James Craig , 17 July 1922
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Hiram Shaw Wilkinson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Condolences paid to Lady Wilson and her response to Queens University condolences

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland
msIdentifier.collection: Correspondence and papers re Field Marshall Sir Henry Wilson, PRONI/CAB 6-89-16

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
CAB6/89/16 PA Moneyshanere,
Tobermore, Co. Derry July 17. 1922. Dear Sir James, Having, as requested, forwarded to
Lady Wilson a message from the recipients
of Honorary Degrees from the Queen's
University of Belfast, conveying to Lady
Wilson the expression of their profound sorrow
at the shocking tragedy which has deprived
her of her illustrious husband, and tendering
to her their respectful sympathy in her great
bereavement, I have now received from Lady
Wilson this accompanying reply, in which she
requests me to convey to the Graduates and all
concerned with Queen's University her deep
appreciation of their tributes to her beloved
husband's memory. Yours sincerely Gentiencinson
